nodes.context: Philadelphia-based African-American organized crime syndicate active from late 1960s to early 1980s, involved in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, etc.
Religious organization; many Black Mafia members converted to Islam, giving the group its nickname.
Italian-American organized crime family in Philadelphia, led by Angelo Bruno during the Black Mafia's rise.
New York mafia family that did business with black organized crime in Philadelphia.
Jamaican gang listed as a rival of the Black Mafia in the infobox.
Successor or splinter group of the Black Mafia, active in a later period.
Philadelphia-based African-American organized crime syndicate active from 1968 to 1984.
Youth gang formed by former Black Mafia members in 1985, active until around 1992.
Italian-American Mafia organization used as a model by the JBM.
Italian-American Mafia crime family in Philadelphia.
Jamaican drug gang involved in a drug war with the JBM.

edges.evidence_quote: Many of the original members eventually became Nation of Islam members or converted to Islam , giving the organization the nickname of "the Muslim Mafia" or "the Muslim Mob."
edges.context: Many Black Mafia members converted to or were members of the Nation of Islam.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Council , Nation of Islam , Philadelphia crime family
edges.context: The infobox lists the Philadelphia crime family as an ally of the Black Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Angelo Bruno , the don of the Philadelphia crime family , discouraged drug dealing in South Philadelphia, but could not prevent deals being made by the New York mafia families such as the Gambino Crime Family , which were doing business with the growing black organized crime that became the Black Mafia
edges.context: The Gambino Crime Family did business with the Black Mafia, supplying drugs.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Shower Posse , and various other gangs in the Philadelphia area, including their allies
edges.context: The infobox lists the Shower Posse as a rival of the Black Mafia.
The Junior Black Mafia is a successor or later iteration of the Black Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1985, members of the original Black Mafia organized African-American youths into the Junior Black Mafia (JBM), also known as the Young Black Mafia, in order to counter and regain territory from New York-based Jamaican posses who had rapidly taken control of drug distribution in the black neighborhoods of Philadelphia.
edges.context: Black Mafia members created the JBM as a successor organization.

edges.evidence_quote: The JBM espoused an amalgam of characteristics which purposely emulated LCN and Black Mafia control over organized crime and its fondness for symbols and ceremony.
edges.context: JBM modeled its organization and methods after La Cosa Nostra.

edges.evidence_quote: The JBM cooperates with associates of the Italian-American Bruno-Scarfo Mafia crime family in the distribution of cocaine and appears to have modeled its criminal methods after that LCN organization as well as the original Black Mafia, relying heavily on violence and extortion to further its drug enterprise.
edges.context: JBM cooperates with Bruno-Scarfo crime family in cocaine distribution.

edges.evidence_quote: The JBM was involved in a drug war with the Shower Posse in Southwest Philadelphia during the 1980s and early 1990s.
edges.context: JBM fought a drug war against the Shower Posse.
